attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.
Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.
Plentiful in the tropics. When it dances, its petals rub together and make a pleasant ringing sound.
Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.
Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head. It won’t miss prey—even those behind it.

attacks.text: Discard a random Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its flowers give off a relaxing fragrance. The stronger its aroma, the healthier the Roselia is.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: After captivating opponents with its sweet scent, it lashes them with its thorny whips.
It uses its whole body to photosynthesize when exposed to sunlight. Its shell is made from hardened soil.
It lives along water in forests. In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Frenzy Plant.

flavorText: Ancient people imagined that beneath the ground dwelt a gigantic Torterra.
Its legs are short. Whenever it stumbles, its stiff antennae clack with a xylophone-like sound.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: By allowing its cry to resonate in the hollow of its belly, it produces a captivating sound.
To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its appearance changes depending on where it evolved. The materials on hand become a part of its body.

attacks.text: Put 1 random Basic Pokémon from your deck onto your Bench.

flavorText: At night, Combee sleep in a group of about a hundred, packed closely together in a lump.
It houses its colony in cells in its body and releases various pheromones to make those grubs do its bidding.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: When you see Leafeon asleep in a patch of sunshine, you’ll know it is using photosynthesis to produce clean air.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The lawn mower is one of the household appliances that led to the development of the Rotom Dex.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.

attacks.text: Take a {R} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.
A common sight in volcanic areas, it slowly slithers around in a constant search for warm places.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Its brittle shell occasionally spouts intense flames that circulate throughout its body.
Its fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly. Even rain can’t extinguish the fire.
It skillfully controls the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

attacks.text: Discard all {R} Energy from this Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: If the convection microwave oven is not working properly, then the Rotom inhabiting it will become lethargic.
It rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food. It sometimes discovers hot springs.

abilities.text: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks from {R} or {W} Pokémon.

flavorText: If it charges at an enemy, the hairs on its back stand up straight. It is very sensitive to sound.

abilities.text: This Pokémon takes −30 damage from attacks from {R} or {W} Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon can be spotted in wall paintings from as far back as 10,000 years ago. For a while, it was thought to have gone extinct.

abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent's Pokémon done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: With cold air that can reach temperatures as low as −328 degrees Fahrenheit, Regice instantly freezes any creature that approaches it.

attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It doesn’t like to be taken care of. It’s difficult to bond with since it won’t listen to its Trainer.
It lives alone, away from others. Apparently, every one of them believes it is the most important.

attacks.text: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It swims as fast as a jet boat. The edges of its wings are sharp and can slice apart drifting ice.
It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: With its flotation sac inflated, it can carry people on its back. It deflates the sac before it dives.
It used to have a shell on its back long ago. This species is closely related to Pokémon like Shellder.

attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: They normally inhabit rocky seashores, but in times of continuous rain, they can sometimes be found in the mountains, far from the sea.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The line running down its side can store sunlight. It shines vividly at night.
With its shining light, it lures its prey close. However, the light also happens to attract ferocious fish Pokémon—its natural predators.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a {F} Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain’s lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.
It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It can control its body temperature at will. This enables it to freeze the moisture in the atmosphere, creating flurries of diamond dust.
This Rotom has entered a washing machine. It nods with satisfaction after it floods the surrounding area.

attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Rotom has entered a refrigerator. It leaps around gleefully after it uses cold air to freeze the area around it.

attacks.text: Discard 3 {W} Energy from this Pokémon. This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, take a {W} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon.

flavorText: It is born with a wondrous power that lets it bond with any kind of Pokémon.
The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.
Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force. Earaches will occur if you get too close.

attacks.text: Discard a {L} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.
The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

attacks.text: Take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra {L} Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.
Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

attacks.text: Discard all {L} Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 40 more damage.
If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.
The shell seems to be filled with joy. It is said that it will share good luck when treated kindly.
They say that it will appear before kindhearted, caring people and shower them with happiness.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overdrive Smash attack does +60 damage.

flavorText: These Pokémon are never seen anywhere near conflict or turmoil. In recent times, they’ve hardly been seen at all.
This Pokémon startles people in the middle of the night. It gathers fear as its energy.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.
It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.
If it finds bad children who won’t listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it’s said.
It seeks drifting will-o’-the-wisps and sucks them into its empty body. What happens inside is a mystery.

abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may choose 1 of your Pokémon that has damage on it, and move all of its damage to this Pokémon.

flavorText: At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

flavorText: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.
Some say this Pokémon is a collection of souls burdened with regrets, silently drifting through the dusk.

attacks.text: Take a {P} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to Mesprit or Azelf.

flavorText: Known as “The Being of Knowledge.” It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you have Uxie and Azelf on your Bench. Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Known as “The Being of Willpower.” It sleeps at the bottom of a lake to keep the world in balance.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: This Pokémon is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours, where common knowledge is distorted and strange.

attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.
Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging tackles.
It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.

attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

flavorText: It can load up to three projectiles per arm into the holes in its hands. What launches out of those holes could be either rocks or Roggenrola.
It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!
It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.
It moves less than an inch a year, but when it’s in a jam, it will spin and drill down into the ground in a split second.

abilities.text: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

flavorText: Every bit of Regirock’s body is made of stone. As parts of its body erode, this Pokémon sticks rocks to itself to repair what’s been lost.
A primeval Pokémon, it possesses a hard and sturdy skull, lacking any intelligence within.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, this Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: In ancient times, people would dig up fossils of this Pokémon and use its skull, which is harder than steel, to make helmets.
Its appearance changes depending on where it evolved. The materials on hand become a part of its body.
They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

abilities.text: Attacks used by your {F} Pokémon do +20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It’s said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras—even those of foes it could not otherwise see.
It shuts its nostrils tight, then travels through sand as if walking. They form colonies of around 10.
It is surprisingly quick to anger. It holds its mouth agape as a display of its strength.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It is merciless by nature. It is said that it never forgives the mistakes of its Murkrow followers.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This cunning Pokémon hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.
It will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.
It sprays a foul fluid from its rear. Its stench spreads over a mile radius, driving Pokémon away.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It attacks by spraying a horribly smelly fluid from the tip of its tail. Attacks from above confound it.

attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.
After burrowing into the sand, it waits patiently for prey to come near. This Pokémon and Sizzlipede share common descent.

attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads. If at least 2 of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its poison is potent, but it rarely sees use. This Pokémon prefers to use physical force instead, going on rampages with its car-crushing strength.

attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Swaying and dodging the attacks of its foes, it weaves its flexible body in close, then lunges out with its poisonous claws.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It chases people and Pokémon from its territory by causing them to experience deep, nightmarish slumbers.

abilities.text: Whenever you attach a {D} Energy from your Energy Zone to this Pokémon, do 20 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: People fashion swords from Skarmory’s shed feathers, so this Pokémon is a popular element in heraldic designs.
Registeel’s body is made of a strange material that is flexible enough to stretch and shrink but also more durable than any metal.
A mild-mannered, herbivorous Pokémon, it used its face to dig up tree roots to eat. The skin on its face was plenty tough.

abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon takes −100 damage from that attack.

flavorText: The bones of its face are huge and hard, so they were mistaken for its spine until after this Pokémon was successfully restored.

attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its appearance changes depending on where it evolved. The materials on hand become a part of its body.
Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor’s back contained a mysterious power.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

flavorText: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Although it can control its units known as Mini-Noses, they sometimes get lost and don’t come back.

attacks.text: Take 2 {M} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.
It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.
In rare cases, it molts and sheds its scales. Medicine containing its scales as an ingredient will make a weary body feel invigorated.

abilities.text: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw a card.

flavorText: It is said that when one runs at high speed, its wings create blades of wind that can fell nearby trees.
If this Pokémon’s sticky saliva gets on you and you don’t clean it off, an intense itch will set in. The itch won’t go away, either.

attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.
State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon. It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.
This is a Porygon that was updated with special data. Porygon2 develops itself by learning about many different subjects all on its own.

attacks.text: Change the type of the next Energy that will be generated for your opponent to 1 of the following at random: {G},{R},{W},{L},{P},{F},{D}, or {M}.

flavorText: Porygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to move between dimensions, but the program also caused instability in Porygon-Z’s behavior.
As it did more and more with its tail, its hands became clumsy. It makes its nest high in the treetops.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It uses its tails for everything. If it wraps both of its tails around you and gives you a squeeze, that’s proof it really likes you.

attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.
Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

attacks.text: Halve your opponent's Active Pokémon's remaining HP, rounded down.

flavorText: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.
It busily makes its nest with stacks of branches and roots it has cut up with its sharp incisors.
If both of Buneary’s ears are rolled up, something is wrong with its body or mind. It’s a sure sign the Pokémon is in need of care.

attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Lopunny is constantly monitoring its surroundings. If danger approaches, this Pokémon responds with superdestructive kicks.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate. Its fickleness is very popular among some.

attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. Choose a card you find there and shuffle it into your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It would claim another Pokémon’s nest as its own if it finds a nest sufficiently comfortable.

attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It mimics the cries of other Pokémon to trick them into thinking it’s one of them. This way they won’t attack it.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: This Rotom has entered an electric fan. It smirks with satisfaction over a prank well pulled after it blows away everything around it.

attacks.text: This attack does more damage equal to the damage this Pokémon has on it.

flavorText: It is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.

trainer.text:
Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.

Play this card as if it were a 40-HP Basic {C} Pokémon.
At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.
This card can't retreat.
Choose a Pokémon in your hand and switch it with a random Pokémon in your deck.
The Pokémon this card is attached to gets +20 HP.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
At the end of each turn, if the Pokémon this card is attached to is affected by any Special Conditions, it recovers from all of them, and discard this card.
Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has damage on it to the Active Spot.
Put 1 random Glameow, Stunky, or Croagunk from your deck into your hand.
During this turn, attacks used by your Garchomp or Togekiss do +50 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Choose 1 of your Electivire or Luxray. Attach 2 {L} Energy from your discard pile to that Pokémon.
Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Your opponent shuffles their hand into their deck and draws a card for each of their remaining points needed to win.
